articles.title: "BE DATA-DRIVEN" IS TERRIBLE ADVICE FOR PMS
articles.summary: PMs today are told to choose data over instincts.

articles.title: YOU SHOULD PLAY WITH VIBECODING FOR GTM
articles.summary: AI coding tools are transforming go-to-market teams by enabling non-technical professionals to quickly build tools like landing pages, ROI calculators, and lead forms. This speeds up execution, reduces dependence on engineering, and unlocks cost-effective, creative solutions to GTM challenges.

articles.title: THE THING YOU ARE EXPERT AT WILL BE YOUR CAREER DOWNFALL
articles.summary: Experts resist AI due to comfort, identity, and craft love, but history shows tech shifts create new expertise. Adaptation involves intentional AI experimentation, broadening identity to outcomes, and embracing AI as a new creative domain. Recognizing AI's strengths while valuing human elements is crucial for evolving expertise.

articles.title: THE ERA OF THE PRODUCT CREATOR
articles.summary: A product manager's core job is to create products - not just facilitate, manage projects, or maintain backlogs. As AI and accessible tools empower more creators, PMs who don't drive product creation risk falling behind in a shifting industry.

articles.title: YOU CATCH EVERY RISK - SO WHY AREN'T YOU IN THE ROOM FOR THE BIG DECISIONS
articles.summary: Effective PMs who excel at finding flaws can be excluded from strategy if their assertiveness is perceived as rigidity. To gain influence, they should shift from solely catching issues to guiding outcomes by emphasizing shared goals, fostering team risk identification, and prioritizing influence, thus becoming seen as strategists.

articles.title: PERFECT TEAMS DON'T EXIST. THAT'S THE GOOD NEWS
articles.summary: The idea of a perfect product team or process is a myth. Success depends on context and constant evolution. Real product success involves driving business value, increasing influence, improving user outcomes, and advancing your career, despite imperfections. Focusing on these areas, rather than chasing an ideal, leads to greater impact.

articles.title: 🥶 WHY COLD STARTS KILL CUSTOMER-FACING AI - AND HOW TO DELIVER REAL-TIME CONTEXT
articles.summary: AI apps have a cold start problem: they feel generic and same-y before they know who users are and what they need. Snowplow Signals [11] combines streaming and historical data to deliver real-time customer intelligence for AI applications. Join the July 29 webinar to

articles.title: learn how to build context-rich AI
HOW TO SOLOPRENEURSHIP IN 2025
articles.summary: To be a successful solopreneur in product and growth, focus on attracting high-value clients through targeted content and networking. Offer skills aligned with your passion, identify gaps, and refine expertise. Monetize by specializing, understanding your value, and respecting your time.

articles.title: YOUR COMPETITORS ARE TELLING YOU THEIR STRATEGY
WHEN TOO MUCH CYA BACKFIRES
articles.summary: Performative CYA behavior can hurt your professional presence, even if it's well-intentioned. Overly formal or deferential language can make you seem insecure and prevent open communication, so it's better to speak with calm confidence and show ownership rather than seeking constant validation.

articles.title: WHY GREAT IDEAS FAIL IN FINANCE: IT'S NOT THE DIGITAL PRODUCT, IT'S THE UX
articles.summary: Banks now compete through apps, where poor UX can cost customers. To stay ahead of fintechs, they must go beyond digitization, using AI and authentic branding to deliver personalized, trustworthy experiences, and make UX a core leadership focus.

articles.title: QUIET AI
articles.summary: Truly impactful AI becomes essential by working reliably in the background, not through showy demonstrations.

articles.title: LAND THE PLANE
articles.summary: In a crisis, leaders must quickly find the cause, define what's "good enough," align the team, and act fast.
